fire_rated.purpose: Maintain fire compartmentation where M&E services penetrate fire-rated walls and floors. Prevent fire and smoke spreading through service openings.
standards: BS EN 1366-3 (fire resistance tests for penetration seals)
BS EN 13501-2 (fire classification of penetration seals)
Approved Document B (Building Regulations — fire safety)
fire_rated.rule: Every service penetration through a fire-rated wall or floor MUST be fire stopped. The fire stop must match the fire rating of the wall/floor it passes through.

intumescent_collar.name: Intumescent Pipe Collar
intumescent_collar.function: Wraps around plastic pipe at fire wall penetration. Expands in fire to crush and seal the pipe opening after the plastic melts.
intumescent_collar.application: Plastic pipes (uPVC, CPVC, ABS, PE) passing through fire-rated construction.
intumescent_collar.brands: Hilti CP 648-S, Quelfire QuelStop, Promat PROMASEAL, Rockwool Conlit
intumescent_collar.sizes: Available for pipe diameters 32mm to 315mm typically. Larger sizes use wrap or compound.
intumescent_collar.note: Intumescent collars are for PLASTIC pipes only. Metal pipes use different fire stop methods (sealant, compound, or wrap).
flag_if: Plastic pipe through fire wall without collar shown
Pipe diameter exceeds collar manufacturer range

fire_rated_wrap.name: Fire-Rated Pipe Wrap / Bandage
fire_rated_wrap.function: Mineral wool or intumescent wrap applied around pipe for a defined length either side of fire wall penetration.
fire_rated_wrap.application: Metal pipes (steel, copper) passing through fire-rated construction. Also used on insulated pipe runs through fire compartments.
fire_rated_wrap.measurement: m (linear) — wrap extends from wall face to specified distance each side (typically 500mm-1500mm per tested system).
specification_required: Wrap length either side of wall (per test certificate)
fire_rated_wrap.note: The wrap length either side of the wall is specified by the manufacturer test certificate. Do NOT assume standard lengths — they vary by system.
flag_if: Fire wrap specified without stating tested system
Wrap extent (length each side) not stated

fire_rated_lagging.name: Fire-Rated Lagging / Insulation
fire_rated_lagging.function: Continuous fire-rated insulation on pipe runs within fire compartments — maintains both thermal and fire performance.
fire_rated_lagging.application: Pipe runs through protected escape routes, fire-rated risers, or where continuous fire rating is required along the pipe (not just at penetrations).
fire_rated_lagging.measurement: m (linear) by pipe size. OR m² if around equipment.
fire_rated_lagging.material: Dense mineral wool (non-combustible, Euroclass A1) with fire-rated facing (foil or intumescent facing).
fire_rated_lagging.note: Different from standard thermal insulation — must be tested and certified for fire rating. Standard mineral wool provides some fire resistance but may not meet specific fire rating requirements without the correct facing and thickness.
fire_rated_lagging.flag_if: Pipe runs within protected escape route without fire-rated insulation specification

firestopping_sleeve.name: Firestopping Sleeve / Pipe Sleeve with Intumescent
firestopping_sleeve.function: Pre-formed sleeve cast into wall/floor during construction. Contains intumescent material that seals in fire.
firestopping_sleeve.application: Multiple services through a single opening. Also used for cable bundles, mixed services.
specification_required: Number and type of services passing through
firestopping_sleeve.note: Sleeves are often installed during the structural/architectural phase (builder's work). M&E contractor installs the fire sealant around services within the sleeve.

fire_sealant.name: Fire-Rated Sealant / Compound
fire_sealant.function: Fills the annular gap between service and sleeve/opening in fire wall. Provides fire and smoke seal.

types.acrylic: Acrylic fire sealant — standard for small gaps around metal pipes and cables. Paintable.
types.intumescent: Intumescent sealant — expands in fire. For larger gaps and plastic pipes.
types.compound: Fire-rated compound / mortar — for large openings with multiple services (fire-rated pillows, batt, or compound).
types.wrap_and_sealant: Mineral wool batt in opening sealed with intumescent sealant on both faces.

fire_sealant.measurement: nr per penetration (typically included in the fire stopping item), or m (linear) for linear gap sealing at service routes.
fire_sealant.brands: Hilti CFS-S, Quelfire Intumescent Sealant, Promat PROMASEAL, 3M Fire Barrier
fire_sealant.note: Fire sealant is the most commonly required fire stopping item. Almost every service penetration needs sealant. Count carefully.

transit_system.name: Fire-Rated Transit System
transit_system.function: Modular frame system for multiple services through a single fire-rated opening. Allows addition/removal of services without remaking the fire stop.
transit_system.brands: Hilti CFS-T, Roxtec, Beele STI ES-Seal, Hawke
transit_system.application: Plant room walls with many penetrations, riser entries, cable transit floors in data centres.
transit_system.measurement: nr per opening frame + nr per module/insert for each service within the frame.
transit_system.note: Transit systems are expensive but allow future service additions without breaching the fire barrier. Increasingly specified on premium commercial projects.

fire_ratings.typical_use: Standard fire compartment walls and floors. Most common rating.
Major fire walls, protected shafts, stair enclosures, high-risk areas.
Specialist — storage of hazardous materials, nuclear, industrial.

count_method.rule: Count EVERY service penetration through a fire-rated wall or floor. Each pipe, each cable, each duct = 1nr fire stop.
count_method.from_drawings: Identify all fire-rated walls/floors on architectural/fire drawings. Count every M&E service crossing each fire barrier.
count_method.from_spec: Spec may state fire stopping requirements per NRM2 WS 31. Cross-reference.
count_method.cross_ref: Fire dampers in ductwork (KB-M03) are a separate item from pipe/cable fire stopping. Both are needed at the same wall if both duct and pipe penetrate.
count_method.note: Fire stopping is one of the most commonly UNDERESTIMATED items in M&E takeoffs. Count methodically.

flag_triggers: Any pipework penetrating fire compartment wall/floor without fire stop detail — flag.
Spec references fire rating without stating minutes — flag.
Drawing shows fire wall penetration without fire stop schedule or detail — flag.
Plastic pipe through fire wall without intumescent collar — flag.
Fire stop product specified without test certificate reference — flag.
Fire rating of fire stop does not match fire rating of wall/floor — flag.
Large opening with multiple services and no transit system or compound detail — flag.

acoustic.purpose: Reduce noise transmission from mechanical services to occupied spaces. Prevent breakout noise from pipes, ducts, and equipment.

acoustic_pipe_lagging.name: Acoustic Pipe Lagging
acoustic_pipe_lagging.function: Reduces noise breakout from pipework (water flow noise, pump vibration transmission).
acoustic_pipe_lagging.material: Dense mineral wool (80-120 kg/m³) with heavy mass barrier layer (loaded vinyl or lead sheet). Often called "mass loaded" or "acoustic lag".
applications: Waste and soil pipes above occupied areas
Water supply risers in residential/hotel walls
Heating pipes in residential party walls
Pipes near bedrooms, meeting rooms, courts, studios
acoustic_pipe_lagging.note: Acoustic pipe lagging is significantly heavier and more expensive than standard thermal insulation. The mass barrier layer is the key acoustic element.
acoustic_pipe_lagging.flag_if: Noise-sensitive areas (bedrooms, courtrooms, studios) with pipes running through — check if acoustic lagging required.

anti_vibration_mount.name: Anti-Vibration Mount / Isolator
anti_vibration_mount.function: Prevents vibration from rotating equipment (pumps, fans, chillers) transmitting through structure to occupied spaces.

types.rubber: Rubber mount / pad — standard for most commercial plant. Simple, cost-effective.
types.spring: Spring isolator — for larger/heavier equipment or where higher isolation efficiency needed.
types.inertia_base: Inertia base (concrete block on springs) — heavy plant on upper floors, near sensitive areas.
types.flexible_connector: Flexible connector on pipework/ductwork — isolates vibration at equipment connection.

anti_vibration_mount.measurement: nr — count per mount point. Check equipment schedule for weight and mount requirements.
anti_vibration_mount.cross_ref: See KB-M04 for mechanical plant associated items.
anti_vibration_mount.note: Anti-vibration is often specified by the acoustic consultant. Check acoustic report for recommendations.

acoustic_enclosure.name: Acoustic Enclosure / Attenuation Chamber
acoustic_enclosure.function: Fully encloses noisy equipment (generators, compressors, pumps) to contain noise.
acoustic_enclosure.measurement: m² (surface area of enclosure panels). Or nr for proprietary packaged enclosures.
acoustic_enclosure.material: Perforated steel outer skin, mineral wool acoustic infill, solid steel inner skin (mass-spring-mass construction).
applications: Pump set enclosure (where noise is critical)
acoustic_enclosure.note: Acoustic enclosures need ventilation — the equipment inside generates heat. Ventilation louvres with acoustic attenuation are part of the enclosure.
acoustic_enclosure.flag_if: Noisy equipment near occupied space without acoustic treatment specified.

acoustic_duct_lining.name: Acoustic Duct Lining
acoustic_duct_lining.function: Internal lining of ductwork for noise attenuation. Absorbs airborne noise within duct.
acoustic_duct_lining.note: Covered in detail in KB-I02 (internal lining). Cross-reference.

sensitive_areas.description: Areas where acoustic insulation is most likely to be required:

areas.note: Very noise-sensitive. Pipes in walls/ceilings must be acoustically lagged.
Party walls, floors above/below. Waste pipes, heating pipes through structure.
Plant rooms adjacent to wards are common problem. Vibration isolation critical.
Particularly near plant rooms and above/below classrooms.
Very strict noise criteria. All services acoustically treated.
Extremely strict. Specialist acoustic design required.
Standard commercial. Less stringent but still relevant near plant.
Very strict during performance. HVAC noise is often the dominant concern.

flag_triggers: Acoustic specification referenced without material type or performance requirement stated — flag.
Plant room adjacent to noise-sensitive area without acoustic treatment specification — flag.
Pipework through hotel/residential party walls without acoustic lagging — flag.
No anti-vibration mounts specified for rotating equipment (pumps, fans, chillers) — flag.
Acoustic enclosure specified without ventilation consideration — flag.

condensation_control.purpose: Prevent surface condensation on cold pipework, equipment, and ductwork. Condensation causes: water damage to ceilings/walls, mould growth, insulation degradation, corrosion.
condensation_control.rule: ANY surface operating below the ambient dew point temperature WILL produce condensation if not properly insulated with a vapour-sealed system.

chilled_water.reason: ChW temperature is well below ambient dew point (typically 12-18°C in UK buildings). Condensation is GUARANTEED without proper insulation.
chilled_water.vapour_barrier: Integral — closed-cell structure provides inherent VB. NO additional VB needed if all joints are properly sealed.
chilled_water.joint_sealing: ALL joints MUST be glued with manufacturer adhesive. Butt joints, longitudinal seams, and branch junctions ALL sealed.
chilled_water.support_inserts: Vapour-sealed pipe support inserts at EVERY bracket/clip. Standard clips compress insulation → cold bridge → condensation → dripping.
chilled_water.thickness: 25-50mm depending on pipe size and ambient conditions. See KB-I01 thickness tables.
flag_if: ChW insulation not specified as closed-cell/elastomeric

cold_water.material: Elastomeric foam — same as ChW but may be thinner (9-25mm depending on ambient).
cold_water.reason: L8 ACOP requires CWS to be maintained below 20°C. In warm plant rooms (25-35°C ambient), uninsulated CWS pipes warm up AND may condensate.
cold_water.thickness: See KB-I01 CWS thickness table. Thicker insulation in warm environments.
flag_if: CWS in plant room or warm ceiling void without insulation
CWS insulation specified as mineral wool without separate VB

refrigerant.material: Elastomeric foam — BOTH liquid and suction lines.
refrigerant.reason: Suction line carries cold refrigerant gas (typically 5-10°C). Will condensate immediately without insulation. Liquid line is warm but still needs insulation for efficiency.
refrigerant.cross_ref: See KB-M03 for VRF/VRV system details.
refrigerant.flag_if: Refrigerant pipe shown without insulation — flag BOTH lines.

condensate.reason: Condensate from cooling coils (AHU, FCU, DX) is cold water. Uninsulated condensate pipe WILL drip.
condensate.note: Condensate insulation is frequently missed in specifications. If cooling equipment is present, condensate pipes should be insulated.
condensate.flag_if: Cooling equipment (AHU, FCU, VRF) shown without condensate pipe insulation specification.

cold_ductwork.material: External wrap with vapour barrier — glass wool foil-faced or elastomeric sheet.
cold_ductwork.reason: Supply air from cooling coil is typically 12-16°C. Duct surface will condensate in warm ceiling void.
cold_ductwork.cross_ref: See KB-I02 for duct insulation details.
cold_ductwork.flag_if: Cold supply air ductwork without external insulation + VB.

vapour_barrier_rules: Vapour barrier must be CONTINUOUS — any break allows moisture migration to cold surface.
ALL joints sealed with manufacturer-specified adhesive or vapour barrier tape.
Pipe support inserts maintain VB continuity at brackets — standard clips break the VB.
Penetrations through VB (sensors, tapping points) must be sealed.
If VB is damaged during installation, it MUST be repaired immediately. Patch with matching material + adhesive.
VB is on the WARM side of the insulation (closest to the ambient air, furthest from the cold pipe).
For elastomeric insulation, the VB is the outer surface of the insulation itself — no separate VB needed if joints are sealed.
common_failures: Unsealed joints in elastomeric insulation — moisture migrates through gap → condensation under insulation → corrosion.
Standard pipe clips crushing insulation — cold bridge at every support point → drip lines on ceiling.
Missing insulation on valves and fittings — cold bridge → localised condensation → dripping.
Insulation damaged during other trade installation — VB broken → condensation.
Insulation installed with gaps at tees and branches — cold spots → condensation.
Wrong material used (mineral wool without VB on cold pipe) — moisture migrates through fibrous insulation → saturated → useless → heavy → falls off.

specialist.rule: Specialist applications require specific materials, finishes, and installation methods. ALWAYS flag for estimator review — costs vary significantly from standard insulation.

catering_food.name: Catering & Food Service Areas
requirements: Hygienic cladding: stainless steel (316L preferred) or smooth sealed PVC. No exposed mineral wool fibres.
All insulation fully sealed against moisture ingress (steam, wash-down water).
Smooth, cleanable external surface — no crevices, no exposed fixings.
Insulation must be food-safe — no contamination risk from insulation material.
All joints sealed with food-grade sealant.
Pest-resistant — no gaps where insects/rodents can nest in insulation.
catering_food.typical_cladding: Stainless steel 316L with hygienic press-stud closures or continuously welded seams.
catering_food.cost_note: Significantly more expensive than standard plant room insulation — 3-5× cost due to stainless steel and hygiene requirements.
catering_food.flag_if: Services in commercial kitchen or food production area without hygienic cladding specification.

pharmaceutical_cleanroom.name: Pharmaceutical / Cleanroom
requirements: Smooth, cleanable, non-shedding surfaces — no exposed mineral wool or glass fibre.
Closed-cell insulation (elastomeric or phenolic with sealed facing) to prevent particle shedding.
All penetrations through clean room boundary sealed to maintain room pressure and cleanliness.
Cladding: stainless steel 316L with welded or sealed joints. Flush with wall/ceiling surfaces.
Minimal exposed fixings — concealed fixing methods preferred.
All insulation materials must be compatible with clean room cleaning agents.

clean_room_classes.ISO_5: Class 100 — very stringent. No exposed insulation of any kind. All services concealed.
clean_room_classes.ISO_7: Class 10,000 — strict. Minimal exposed services. Sealed smooth cladding where exposed.
clean_room_classes.ISO_8: Class 100,000 — moderate. Similar to food production requirements.

pharmaceutical_cleanroom.cost_note: Clean room insulation is specialist — 5-10× standard cost. Always flag for specialist quotation.
pharmaceutical_cleanroom.flag_if: Services in clean room or pharmaceutical area without specialist insulation specification.

external_weather.name: External / Weathering Protection
requirements: All insulation protected from UV degradation — UV destroys most insulation materials.
Weather-resistant cladding: aluminium (standard), stainless steel (coastal/corrosive), or UV-stable PVC jacket.
All joints and end caps sealed with weather-resistant sealant (silicone or butyl).
Prevent water ingress — water in insulation destroys thermal performance and causes corrosion.
Slope cladding joints to shed water (horizontal runs: joints on top, water runs off sides).
End caps at every termination to prevent water entering insulation from pipe/duct ends.

typical_cladding.standard: Aluminium stucco embossed, 0.8mm gauge (thicker than internal). Pop-riveted with sealant on laps.
typical_cladding.coastal: Stainless steel 316 or marine-grade aluminium. Standard aluminium corrodes in coastal environments.
typical_cladding.industrial: Aluminium or stainless steel with enhanced sealant and banding.

external_weather.cost_note: External insulation is 1.5-2× internal cost due to heavier cladding, sealant, and weatherproofing.
flag_if: Coastal location without stainless steel or marine-grade cladding specification

high_temperature.name: High Temperature (>120°C: Steam, HTHW)
requirements: Standard elastomeric and phenolic insulation CANNOT be used above 120°C.
Material: calcium silicate sections (up to 650°C) or high-temperature mineral wool (up to 300°C+).
Double-layer insulation may be required for very high temperatures (>200°C) — inner layer high-temp, outer layer standard.
Personal protection cladding: where insulated surface temperature exceeds 60°C, outer cladding must bring surface below 60°C to prevent burns.
Expansion allowance: high-temperature pipes expand significantly. Insulation must accommodate expansion without cracking or opening gaps.
Stainless steel or aluminium cladding — painted mild steel also acceptable for internal.

personal_protection.rule: If the OUTER SURFACE of the insulation/cladding exceeds 60°C, it is a burn hazard. Spec should require sufficient insulation thickness to bring surface below 60°C.
personal_protection.standard: HSE guidance — surface temperatures >60°C require either: increased insulation, warning labels, or physical barriers.
personal_protection.flag_if: High temperature system (steam, HTHW >120°C) without personal protection assessment.

high_temperature.cost_note: High-temperature insulation is 2-4× standard cost. Calcium silicate is significantly more expensive and heavier than mineral wool.
flag_if: Steam or HTHW system without high-temp insulation spec
No personal protection assessment for high-temp pipework in accessible areas

trace_heating.name: Trace Heating + Insulation
trace_heating.function: Electric heating cable/tape applied to pipe surface under insulation. Prevents freezing or maintains process temperature.
applications: Frost protection for cold water in unheated spaces

components.heating_cable: Self-regulating or constant wattage cable. Measured in m (linear) along pipe. W/m rating per spec.
components.thermostat: Ambient or pipe-mounted thermostat controlling trace heating. Count as nr.
components.junction_box: Power connection and termination boxes. Count as nr.
components.insulation: ALWAYS required over trace heating — the insulation retains the heat. Without insulation, trace heating is ineffective.
components.power_supply: Dedicated electrical circuit for trace heating. Cross-reference KB-E.

trace_heating.measurement: Trace heating cable: m (linear). Thermostats and junction boxes: nr. Insulation over trace heating: m (per KB-I01).
trace_heating.note: Trace heating is a COMBINED mechanical + electrical + insulation scope item. Ensure all three elements are counted.
flag_if: Trace heating specified without insulation over it
External pipework in frost-risk area without trace heating or drain-down provision
Trace heating electrical supply not addressed

flag_triggers: Service penetration through fire-rated wall/floor without fire stopping detail — flag as CRITICAL.
Fire rating of fire stop not stated or does not match wall/floor rating — flag.
Plastic pipe through fire wall without intumescent collar — flag.
Acoustic treatment not specified for plant adjacent to noise-sensitive areas — flag.
Pipework through hotel/residential party walls without acoustic lagging — flag.
Cold pipework without closed-cell insulation and vapour barrier — flag condensation risk.
Chilled water valve/flange insulation not addressed — flag cold bridge risk.
Condensate pipe insulation not specified — flag dripping risk.
Services in food/catering area without hygienic cladding — flag.
Services in clean room without specialist insulation specification — flag.
External insulation without weatherproof cladding — flag.
Coastal location without marine-grade cladding — flag corrosion risk.
High temperature system (>120°C) with standard insulation material — flag wrong material.
Personal protection not assessed for high-temperature pipework in accessible areas — flag safety.
Trace heating specified without insulation over it — flag ineffective system.
External pipework without frost protection (trace heating or insulation) — flag freeze risk.
Fire stopping count appears low relative to number of fire walls on architectural drawings — flag potential undercount.
